title: Interior channel cable and tube management bracket
abstract: A cable bracket having at least one cable support member and a subtending continuous fastener passageway with a first open end and a second open end spaced from the first open end is disclosed. The first and second open ends are oriented on the same side of the cable bracket to facilitate installation.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A cable bracket, comprising: a. a body having multiple sides; b. A first passageway disposed within the body, the first passageway having a first open end, a second open end spaced from the first open end and a continuous passageway surface interconnecting the first and second open ends, and wherein the first and second open ends are disposed on a common side of the body; and c. a cable support member positioned between the first and second open ends of the channel. 2. The cable bracket of Claim 1, wherein the continuous passageway surface is curved. 3. The cable bracket of Claim 1, wherein the cable support member comprises a contoured surface configured to support a cable. 4. The cable bracket of Claim 2, wherein the contoured surface comprises a curved surface. 5. The cable bracket of Claim 1, wherein the first passageway is fully enclosed, except for the first and second open ends. 6. The cable bracket of Claim 4, wherein the cable support member forms at least a part of the enclosure of the first passageway. 7. The cable bracket of Claim 1, further comprising: a. a second passageway disposed in the body and spaced from the first passageway, the second passageway having a first open end, a second open end spaced from the first open end and a continuous passageway surface interconnecting the first and second open ends, and wherein the first and second open ends of the second passageway are disposed on a common side of the body; and b. a second cable support member positioned between the first and second open ends of the second passageway. 8. The cable bracket of Claim 7, wherein the first and second openings of the first and second passageways are disposed on a common side of the body. 9. The cable bracket of Claim 7, wherein the body is elongated and is curved along its length. 10. The cable bracket of Claim 1, wherein the cable support member comprises a first portion and a second portion, wherein the first portion and second portion define a length of the cable support member, and wherein the first portion is tapered along its length. 11. The cable bracket of Claim 1, further comprising at least one mounting bracket configured to secure the body to a separate structure. 12. The cable bracket of Claim 1, wherein the cable support member has a first end portion, second end portion and a middle portion between the first and second end portions, and wherein the first and second end portions are wider than the middle portion. 13. The cable bracket of Claim 12, wherein the first and second end portions and the middle portion form a continuous curved surface configured to receive a cable. 14. The cable bracket of Claim 7, wherein the first and second passageways are horizontally spaced apart when the bracket is installed. 15. A cable bracket, comprising: a. A first body portion having multiple sides; b. A first channel formed in one side of the first body portion and defining a first side wall and a second side wall along opposite sides of the first channel, the channel having a first end, a second end and a continuous surface interconnecting the first end and second end; and c. A cable support member extending across the first channel from the first side wall to the second side wall. 16. The cable bracket of Claim 15, wherein the first and second side walls have an exterior surface, and the cable support member has an exterior surface, and wherein the exterior surface of the cable support member is continuous with at least a portion of the exterior surface of the first and second side walls. 17. The cable bracket of Claim 16, wherein the exterior surface of the cable support member and the exterior surface of the first and second side walls that are continuous are concave. 18. The cable bracket of Claim 17, wherein the continuous concave surfaces of the cable support member, the first wall and second side wall have the same radius of curvature. 19. The cable bracket of Claim 15, further comprising, a. a second body portion having multiple sides; 

 b. a second channel formed in one side of the second body portion and defining a third side wall and a fourth side wall along opposite sides of the second channel, the second channel having a first end, a second end and a continuous surface interconnecting the first end and the second end; and c. a second cable support member extending across the second channel from the third sidewall to the fourth side wall. 20. The cable bracket of Claim 15, wherein the first and second body portions are vertically spaced apart when the bracket is installed.
